summary:
Given a reference to a node in a connected, undirected graph, produce a deep copy of the whole graph. Every node holds an integer value and a list of its neighbors, and your copy must contain brand-new node objects with the same values and the same connection structure.

In this app the graph is encoded as an adjacency list: index i (1-based node value) maps to the list of values it is connected to. A correct clone reproduces the identical adjacency list.

examples.explanation: Node 1 connects to 2 and 4, node 2 connects to 1 and 3, and so on; the cloned graph has the same four nodes and edges.
A two-node graph where node 1 and node 2 are neighbors of each other.

constraints: Node values are unique and equal to the node's 1-based index
The graph is undirected and connected (the empty graph returns an empty result)
The returned graph must use entirely new node objects, not the originals
